skill.name: rn-observability
skill.description: Logging, error messages, and debugging patterns for React Native. Use when adding logging, designing error messages, debugging production issues, or improving code observability.
skill.summary: Improve React Native diagnostics with structured logging, useful errors, redaction, tracing, and production reporting patterns.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: The high static score is not confirmed. SKILL.md is a Markdown guide with TypeScript examples; command execution, sensitive file, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from code fences, template literals, and ordinary words. The retained risk is low because copied logging and Sentry examples could expose user data without redaction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The static command findings point to Markdown code fences and TypeScript template literals in documentation examples. No shell execution, Ruby backticks, or executable skill script was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are Markdown fences, inline code, or TypeScript template strings. I found no executable file or instruction that runs shell commands.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Sensitive File Alerts Are Logging Examples
low_findings.description: The sensitive findings refer to example variables and Object.keys calls inside diagnostic logging guidance. They do not reference certificate files, private keys, or local credential paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains no certificate or key material. The surrounding context is example code for counting object keys and redacting sensitive field names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Alerts Match Non-Crypto Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to descriptive text and a string-matching redaction example. No cryptographic API, hash algorithm, cipher, or key generation logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose headings or a redaction condition. They do not contain MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any crypto call.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Alert Is a Comment
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance finding points to a comment in a bad early-return example. No system information command or environment inspection pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a human-readable comment inside a TypeScript example. It does not run or request reconnaissance commands.

low_findings.title: Privacy-Sensitive Logging Guidance Requires Redaction
low_findings.description: The guide recommends diagnostic context, state snapshots, stack traces, and Sentry extras. This is legitimate observability guidance, but implementations must redact secrets and personal data before logging or reporting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The privacy concern is directly supported by the logging and production error reporting examples. The risk remains low because the same document includes explicit redaction guidance.

content.value_statement: Silent failures and weak error messages make React Native production issues hard to diagnose. This skill provides logging, redaction, tracing, and error reporting patterns that make failures easier to investigate.
actual_capabilities: Reviews early returns and recommends contextual log messages.
Improves error messages with function names, failed conditions, state, and likely causes.
Provides structured logging patterns with debug, info, warn, and error levels.
Shows how to redact sensitive fields before logging diagnostic context.
Adds flow tracing, state snapshots, assertions, and production error reporting patterns.
limitations: Provides guidance and examples, not a ready-made observability library.
Requires adaptation to the project's logger, state store, and error reporting service.
Does not configure Sentry, dashboards, alerts, or log ingestion pipelines.
Cannot guarantee privacy compliance unless teams review logged fields.

use_cases.title: Add Logs to a Bug Fix
use_cases.description: Use the skill to add clear logs around early returns, async steps, and state changes in a React Native fix.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Diagnostics
use_cases.description: Use the skill to add error reporting context, flow identifiers, and stack capture for mobile production issues.

use_cases.title: Review Privacy in Logs
use_cases.description: Use the skill to identify sensitive fields and add redaction before logs or crash reports leave the device.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Logging
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single function.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-observability to review this React Native function. Add useful logs for early returns and errors without logging sensitive data.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Error Messages
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-observability to rewrite these error messages so they include location, failed condition, relevant state, and a likely cause.

prompt_templates.title: Trace an Async Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-observability to add flow tracing to this async React Native operation. Include a flow identifier, step logs, and failure context.

prompt_templates.title: Design Production Reporting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use rn-observability to design a production error reporting approach for this feature. Include redaction, log levels, state snapshots, and Sentry context.

output_examples.input: Review a save answer function that sometimes drops user input.
output_examples.output: A diagnostic review that identifies silent return paths, recommends warning logs, and lists safe context fields for each branch.

output_examples.input: Improve a vague React Native error message.
output_examples.output: A rewritten error message plan with function name, failed condition, counts, identifiers, and a short likely cause.

output_examples.input: Prepare an async assessment flow for production debugging.
output_examples.output: A flow tracing plan with step names, flow identifiers, state snapshot points, redaction requirements, and reporting boundaries.

best_practices: Log why each early return happens and include only diagnostic fields.
Redact secrets and personal data before console logs or error reporting.
Use flow identifiers for multi-step operations so related logs are searchable.
anti_patterns: Do not log whole user objects, tokens, answers, or payment data.
Do not swallow caught errors without a warning or error log.
Do not leave temporary debug logging enabled without a gate or cleanup plan.

faq.question: Does this skill install a logging library?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns that you adapt to your existing React Native logger and tooling.

faq.question: Can it help with Sentry?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows how to capture errors with context, but you must configure Sentry separately.

faq.question: Is it safe for production apps?
faq.answer: Yes, if teams apply the redaction guidance and avoid logging secrets or personal data.

faq.question: What problems does it target?
faq.answer: It targets silent returns, vague errors, unstructured logs, missing flow traces, and weak production diagnostics.

faq.question: Does it replace observability platforms?
faq.answer: No. It improves application code so existing platforms receive clearer and safer diagnostic context.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, or Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
